one_line_ask: Take the client's three raster logo-concept renders and turn them into a clean, scalable brand-asset set: background-removed transparent masters, a color-corrected primary, a one-color (single-ink) version, a circular badge crop, and a vectorized SVG — all assembled on a Firefly board for sign-off.
brief:
Maple Ridge Home Services is launching a premium-but-warm home-services brand (tagline: "Clean. Caring. Consistent.") and has early concept artwork it needs elevated into a polished, scalable asset system. The client supplies three raster files: (1) a photographed full-color primary lockup (maple-leaf icon + wordmark) shot on a cream studio sweep, (2) the maple-leaf-only icon photographed on a cluttered wooden workshop desk (busy background that must be stripped), and (3) a circular-badge concept render. The freelancer must NOT redraw the mark — the work is to clean, color-correct, and productize the existing concepts into the exact deliverables the brief lists.

Pipeline: ingest the three client files into a working folder; straighten and tightly crop the primary lockup; auto-tone then warm/normalize its color toward the requested warm olive-sage / muted-terracotta / cream palette and lift the cream background to a clean even ivory; isolate the maple-leaf icon from its busy desk shot via prompt-driven subject selection and background removal to a transparent PNG; from that clean transparent icon, derive a one-color (single-ink terracotta) version using a flat color overlay for use
